steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content: The previous sentence says the user "first tags the photo with all known information." The word "first" signals a sequence: something comes after this step. The blank must introduce that next step — CWPS's facial-recognition software analyzing the photo. The transition must mean "next in sequence."

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Then,': "Then" is the natural sequential counterpart to "first" — it marks what happens after the tagging step, which is exactly what the second sentence describes. ✓
  • Choice B — 'In fact,': "In fact" emphasizes or clarifies the previous statement rather than advancing a sequence — the second sentence introduces a new step, not a restatement. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Likewise,': "Likewise" signals a parallel or similar action, but tagging and facial-recognition analysis are different steps in a process, not parallel ones. ✗
  • Choice D — 'For example,': "For example" introduces an illustration of the previous point — but analyzing physical features is not an example of tagging; it is the next step. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
